risk factors such as excessive bleeding which often complicates management strategies involving anticoagulation therapies among oncology populations suffering from deep vein thromboses or pulmonary emboli secondary to malignancy progression stages requiring aggressive yet judicious pharmacological interventions targeting coagulopathies linked directly back towards primary tumor burden control presentations indicative thereof across various disease states encompassing different phases ranging early through advanced metastasized forms impacting overall prognosis outcomes accordingly when providers tasked therewith delivering optimal levels of compassionate bedside manner coupled seamlessly together with rigorous scientific rigor applied consistently over time ensuring highest possible
